title: Aegis-OS Audit Event
description: Immutable, structured audit event emitted by AuditLogger at every governance stage, lifecycle transition, and policy decision. This schema is the open standard v0.1 output of the Aegis Governance Loop.

level.description: Structlog severity level emitted by the add_log_level processor.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the event was emitted (e.g. '2026-03-04T10:00:00.000000Z').

task_id.description: UUID of the orchestration task that generated this event.

requester_id.description: Stable identity of the human or service that initiated the agent task.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the specific agent instance emitting a security-relevant event.

action.description: Action being performed or that was attempted (e.g. 'llm.complete', 'token.issue').

resource.description: Resource URI or name that the action targets (e.g. 'model:gpt-4o-mini').

error_message.description: Human-readable error description. Present when outcome is 'error' or 'failure'.

pii_types.description: PII class labels detected and redacted (e.g. ['email', 'ssn']). Present when outcome is 'redact'.

model.description: LLM model identifier. Present in the llm-invoke stage.

tokens_used.description: Token count returned by the LLM adapter. Present in the llm-invoke stage.

error.description: Human-readable error description when an exception or failure is logged.

reasons.description: OPA policy denial reasons or other structured rationale strings.

fields.description: Field names targeted by a policy masking operation (e.g. ['prompt']).

jti.description: JWT unique token identifier (jti claim) from the JIT session token.

budget_session_id.description: UUID of the budget-enforcement session associated with this event.

token_agent_type.description: Agent type encoded inside the presented JIT session token.

request_agent_type.description: Agent type declared by the current in-flight request (may differ from token).

message.description: Free-form human-readable message for lifecycle events such as startup and shutdown.

workflow_id.description: Temporal workflow identifier for events emitted by the Scheduler.

metadata.description: Arbitrary string key-value pairs attached to the request or event.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number within a task run — used for gap and duplicate detection.

stage.description: Named pipeline stage at which this event was emitted (e.g. 'pre-pii-scrub', 'policy-eval').
